primary_target: B0 -> K*0 mu+ mu- A_FB(q^2) zero-crossing
secondary_target: micro-plateau residual nodes in P'_5, dB/dq^2, or A_FB(q^2)
claim_boundary: pre-registered rare-B observable screen; no q0^2 prediction claimed; no institutional endorsement; no full BHSM completion

depends_on.v4_5: lambda_i = w_i/(6*pi^2) remains a conditional spectral covariance candidate
depends_on.v4_6: L_i(rho) whitened boundary operator remains conditional

observable_interface.benchmark_note: The A_FB zero-crossing is comparatively clean with reduced hadronic sensitivity relative to many other rare-B observables, but it is not treated as uncertainty-free.

bhsm_prediction.blocked_statement: BHSM currently lacks an artifact-backed rare-B observable map from its geometric mode/transport structure to the A_FB(q^2) zero-crossing. No forward q0^2 prediction is claimed.

microplateau_prediction.blocked_statement: Micro-plateau structure remains qualitative/exploratory and is not a BHSM prediction.

validated: A_FB zero-crossing identified as a high-value rare-B forward-prediction target
observable interface localized: B0 -> K*0 mu+ mu-, A_FB(q^2), and q0^2 where A_FB(q0^2)=0
micro-plateau residuals identified as secondary exploratory targets only if exact nodes are derivable
invalidated_or_downgraded: qualitative bumpy residual language is not a prediction without exact node coordinates
a geometric metaphor of plasma over lattice is not enough to compute A_FB(q^2)
a correct CKM matrix alone does not automatically provide a rare-B observable map
blocked gauge-coupling normalization cannot be smuggled into rare-B predictions
no q0^2 placeholder or empirical offset is acceptable as a forward prediction
forbidden_claims: claiming a BHSM A_FB zero result without q0^2 value or exact coordinate
claiming micro-plateaus without exact node coordinates
using existing rare-B data to fit the prediction
claiming a derived CKM coefficient value
claiming continuous QFT falsification by BHSM
